title: A GIS-based interactive map for decision-making in Nigeria's food supply chain
author: Odion, Shoji, et al.

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: The web map comprises several GIS layers providing relevant information with actionable insights, the synergy of having all these data available is huge for decision-makers. These layers include elevation, electrical grid, population, air temperature, solar radiation, precipitation, road network, market locations, mobile coverage, land cover, agro-ecological zones, food insecurity, blue water scarcity, water access, and crop production. This paper discusses the process of building an openly accessible Google Earth Engine (GEE) application. We highlight each layer on the web map and its sources and go into detail on the processing steps undergone in transforming the raw datasets into layers ready to convey information to stakeholders.

steps.title: Data preprocessing steps
steps.instruction: The open-source data we collected had various spatial coverage, temporal timespan, and data formats. The processing steps briefly discussed below were necessary to take these differences into account in order to produce layers to be displayed on the web map layer.
